source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.topic: Measures, perimeter, time, angles and shape
source_alignment.source_statement: Measure, compare, add and subtract lengths, mass and volume or capacity; measure the perimeter of simple 2-D shapes; tell and write the time using 12-hour and 24-hour clocks and compare durations; draw 2-D shapes and make 3-D shapes; recognise angles as a property of shape or a description of a turn, identify right angles and compare other angles with a right angle; and identify horizontal, vertical, perpendicular and parallel lines.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack connects practical measuring, timelines, turns and shape construction through explicit unit choice and reference models. Money is intentionally left to number-and-money practice so this review pack can gather coherent evidence across magnitude, duration and spatial reasoning.

objective.child_goal: I can choose measures, work out perimeter and time, and describe angles, lines and shapes.
objective.statement: Select and use standard units, compare and calculate measures and durations, find simple perimeters, and reason about turns, right angles, line relationships and properties of 2-D and 3-D shapes.
misconceptions: Chooses a unit because its number looks convenient, such as measuring a doorway in millimetres or a pencil in metres.
Finds perimeter by counting only some sides, counting corner points, or confusing perimeter with the space inside a shape.
Reads the minute hand as an hour number, treats 3:45 as 3 hours 45 minutes after midnight in every context, or subtracts clock digits without tracking elapsed time.
Thinks a right angle must point in one direction, calls any sharp corner a right angle, or confuses parallel and perpendicular lines.

vocabulary.child_explanation: An agreed amount used to measure something.
vocabulary.audio_script: Choose a unit that suits the size and the thing being measured.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A millimetre mark, centimetre cube, metre stick, gram mass, kilogram mass and litre container.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The total distance all the way around the outside of a 2-D shape.
vocabulary.audio_script: Perimeter is a length, so add every outside side once and include a length unit.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A bright route tracing once around a rectangle and returning to its start.

vocabulary.audio_script: Duration is the amount of time from the start to the finish.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A start marker and finish marker joined by jumps on a timeline.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A square corner or a quarter turn.
vocabulary.audio_script: A right angle is one quarter of a full turn, even when the corner is rotated.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A movable square-corner checker fitted into differently turned corners.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Lines that stay the same distance apart and do not meet.
vocabulary.audio_script: Parallel lines keep the same gap between them.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two straight railway tracks with equal-gap markers.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Lines that meet at a right angle.
vocabulary.audio_script: Perpendicular lines cross or meet to make a square corner.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A vertical line and horizontal line meeting beside a right-angle marker.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Would you measure a pencil in millimetres, centimetres or metres? Estimate first, then check.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Reactivate benchmark lengths and separate unit choice from calculation.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Think about the object's size, choose a sensible unit and make an estimate before measuring.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A pencil sits beside one-millimetre marks, centimetre cubes and a metre stick.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Plan an explorer route by measuring supplies, journey times and the boundary of a camp.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Connect different measures while keeping each quantity and unit distinct.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Length, mass, capacity and time measure different things. The unit tells us what each number means.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: An expedition board groups a route, parcel, water bottle, clock and camp boundary under labelled measure cards.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Trace every outside side of a 6 cm by 4 cm rectangle and find its perimeter.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model perimeter as a complete boundary length rather than area or corner count.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Add six, four, six and four. The perimeter is twenty centimetres because every outside side is included once.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A route starts at one corner, traces four labelled sides and closes the loop beside 6 + 4 + 6 + 4 = 20 cm.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Show 14:35 on the clock, then use a timeline to find the finish time after 50 minutes.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Coordinate 12-hour and 24-hour notation with efficient elapsed-time jumps.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Fourteen thirty-five is two thirty-five in the afternoon. Jump twenty-five minutes to three o'clock, then twenty-five more.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Linked analogue and digital clocks sit above a timeline split at the next hour.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Rotate the right-angle checker. Which corners are right angles, and which line pair is parallel?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Detect orientation-dependent angle judgements and line-language confusion.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The checker can turn without changing its angle. Parallel lines keep the same gap; perpendicular lines meet at a right angle.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A shape board contains rotated corners and line pairs with non-colour texture labels.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Explain how you know the route measure, perimeter, finish time and line description are sensible.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Gather linked explanation evidence without allowing success in one strand to hide insecurity in another.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Name the quantity, unit or property you used, then show the check that supports your answer.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The learner pins one evidence card to each of four expedition decisions.

manipulatives.purpose: Estimate, measure, convert within taught unit pairs and compare length, mass and capacity using visible benchmarks.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners choose a quantity and unit, align a virtual tool at zero, read a marked scale, or use keyboard steppers; comparisons retain both values and units and conversions use grouped 10, 100 or 1000 structures only where taught.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Scales have enlarged labels, spoken tick values, strong major/minor tick contrast and numeric-entry alternatives; objects snap to a labelled zero point; mass and capacity have non-visual tables; dragging is optional; and no judgement depends on colour or fine pointer placement.

manipulatives.purpose: Build shape boundaries, track elapsed time and test angle and line properties with linked representations.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners construct grid shapes, mark each boundary side, move along a time line, rotate a right-angle checker and classify horizontal, vertical, parallel and perpendicular line pairs.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Every spatial task has ordered side lists or coordinate descriptions; clocks expose separate hour and minute values; timelines accept typed starts and jumps; angle rotation uses buttons and spoken quarter-turn labels; line relationships use text and tactile-style patterns as well as orientation.

practice.interleaving_notes: Establish quantity and unit meaning before mixing measures. Teach perimeter, elapsed time and angle/line properties in separate short sequences, then interleave them with an explicit 'What are we measuring or describing?' prompt.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Choose sensible units and measure or compare length, mass and capacity with benchmark support.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: age-familiar object or container + quantity prompt + unit choice + estimate range + aligned scale reading or comparison
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Verify plausible real-world ranges, labelled scale intervals and exact answer uniqueness; avoid inaccessible visual estimation and culturally narrow objects.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Find and compare perimeters of simple rectilinear and labelled 2-D shapes.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: closed shape + side labels or square grid + trace-all-sides checkpoint + perimeter expression + unit
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Ensure every needed side is known or derivable, distinguish grid-square area from boundary units and accept equivalent addition expressions.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Read 12-hour and 24-hour times and calculate durations across hour boundaries.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: analogue/digital start or finish + morning/afternoon context + duration in minutes + split-jump timeline + inverse check
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Check 24-hour conversions, avoid ambiguous noon/midnight contexts and expose the chosen elapsed-time path rather than rewarding speed.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Recognise quarter, half, three-quarter and full turns, compare angles with a right angle and classify line relationships.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: rotated shape or route + turn instruction + right-angle comparator + horizontal/vertical/parallel/perpendicular statement
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Randomise orientation, never encode correctness through colour, and verify line pairs are mathematically unambiguous within the diagram.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Retain unit benchmarks, perimeter, time and geometric property language through short mixed review.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review queue weighted to prior misconception + one representation + one answer-and-check prompt
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep one principal construct per item, include spoken and linearised alternatives, and do not use speed scoring.

body.prompt: Choose the most sensible unit for the length of a pencil, then read its length on the aligned ruler.
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: A pencil is much shorter than a metre.
Check that the pencil starts at zero, then read its end point.
question_variants.explanation: Centimetres are a sensible unit for a pencil. Its end aligns with 16, so the length is 16 cm.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: 16; unit: "centimetres". Centimetres are a sensible unit for a pencil. Its end aligns with 16, so the length is 16 cm.
feedback.repair: A pencil is much shorter than a metre. Check that the pencil starts at zero, then read its end point. Use that clue to repair unit chosen by number, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this measure and compare, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: 16; unit: "centimetres": Centimetres are a sensible unit for a pencil. Its end aligns with 16, so the length is 16 cm.
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: A rectangular camp is 6 m long and 4 m wide. Find the total perimeter and select the matching boundary calculation.
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Trace all the way around and return to the starting corner.
Include both lengths and both widths.
question_variants.explanation: The perimeter includes all four outside sides: 6 + 4 + 6 + 4 = 20 m.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: 20; unit: "metres"; calculation: "6 + 4 + 6 + 4". The perimeter includes all four outside sides: 6 + 4 + 6 + 4 = 20 m.
feedback.repair: Trace all the way around and return to the starting corner. Include both lengths and both widths. Use that clue to repair perimeter partial or area, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this perimeter builder, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: 20; unit: "metres"; calculation: "6 + 4 + 6 + 4": The perimeter includes all four outside sides: 6 + 4 + 6 + 4 = 20 m.
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: The expedition starts at 14:35 and lasts 50 minutes. What time does it finish?
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Jump 25 minutes to 15:00.
There are 25 minutes left to add.
question_variants.explanation: From 14:35 to 15:00 is 25 minutes, then another 25 minutes reaches 15:25.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "15:25". From 14:35 to 15:00 is 25 minutes, then another 25 minutes reaches 15:25.
feedback.repair: Jump 25 minutes to 15:00. There are 25 minutes left to add. Use that clue to repair clock digits subtracted, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this time line, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: "15:25": From 14:35 to 15:00 is 25 minutes, then another 25 minutes reaches 15:25.
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: A square corner has been rotated. Choose its angle and the relationship of a vertical side meeting a horizontal side.
line_choices: do not meet and are not parallel
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Rotate the checker to fit the corner.
Lines that meet in a right angle are perpendicular.
question_variants.explanation: Rotating a corner does not change its angle. A quarter turn is a right angle, and lines meeting there are perpendicular.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is angle: "right angle"; line relationship: "perpendicular". Rotating a corner does not change its angle. A quarter turn is a right angle, and lines meeting there are perpendicular.
feedback.repair: Rotate the checker to fit the corner. Lines that meet in a right angle are perpendicular. Use that clue to repair right angle orientation, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this angle line sort, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports angle: "right angle"; line relationship: "perpendicular": Rotating a corner does not change its angle. A quarter turn is a right angle, and lines meeting there are perpendicular.
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Survey-tool mission 1: choose a sensible unit and instrument for pencil.
body.supported_interaction: An adult or peer may read, scan, set the learner's named tool value or record an indicated explanation without supplying the measure or property.
body.ruler_scale_route: Enlarged labelled major/minor ticks, snap-to-zero, numeric entry and a linear tick table replace precise dragging.
body.mass_capacity_route: Balance masses and measuring-jug marks have object, numeric and non-visual table alternatives.
body.clock_timeline_route: Hour and minute values are separate; static timelines accept typed or selected jumps and show morning/afternoon context.
body.shape_property_route: Rotatable checker, face/edge/vertex cards and linearised side/line descriptions avoid orientation and visual-only demands.
body.visual_route: One active instrument or property panel, high-contrast labelled ticks, large values, generous spacing and no colour-only meaning.
body.processing_route: Reveal estimate, measure/describe and check stages separately; preserve correct sides, jumps and property labels.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, numeric entry, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed values can replace dragging, rotating, speech and handwriting.

gamification.mission: prepare one calm expedition survey decision
gamification.reward: one evidence pin for a checked measure, route, time or property
gamification.retry_message: Your correct measurements and checks stay. Choose another tool or clue and continue.

hints: First identify the quantity: length.
Compare pencil with a known cm benchmark.
question_variants.explanation: cm and a ruler suit the length of pencil; a plausible value is around 16 cm.

feedback.correct: Quantity, unit and instrument matched sensibly.
feedback.repair: Name the quantity first, compare the object with one visible unit benchmark and remove instruments for other quantities.
feedback.mathematical_evidence: cm and a ruler suit the length of pencil; a plausible value is around 16 cm.
feedback.strategy_message: Estimating, measuring, numeric entry, tracing, timeline jumps, pointing, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed explanations are equally valid; speed and drawing precision are not scored.

hints: An estimate is a sensible range, not a hurried exact answer.

body.ruler_scale_route: Enlarged labelled major/minor ticks, snap-to-zero, numeric entry and a linear tick table replace precise dragging.
body.mass_capacity_route: Balance masses and measuring-jug marks have object, numeric and non-visual table alternatives.
body.clock_timeline_route: Hour and minute values are separate; static timelines accept typed or selected jumps and show morning/afternoon context.
body.shape_property_route: Rotatable checker, face/edge/vertex cards and linearised side/line descriptions avoid orientation and visual-only demands.
body.visual_route: One active instrument or property panel, high-contrast labelled ticks, large values, generous spacing and no colour-only meaning.
body.processing_route: Reveal estimate, measure/describe and check stages separately; preserve correct sides, jumps and property labels.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, numeric entry, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed values can replace dragging, rotating, speech and handwriting.

gamification.mission: prepare one calm expedition survey decision

hints: Mark one starting corner and trace all the way around.
Add each outside side once and include a length unit.

feedback.strategy_message: Estimating, measuring, numeric entry, tracing, timeline jumps, pointing, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed explanations are equally valid; speed and drawing precision are not scored.

body.shape_property_route: Rotatable checker, face/edge/vertex cards and linearised side/line descriptions avoid orientation and visual-only demands.

body.motor_alternative: Tap, numeric entry, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed values can replace dragging, rotating, speech and handwriting.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Chooses a unit because its number looks convenient, such as measuring a doorway in millimetres or a pencil in metres.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner selects an implausible unit or gives a scale reading without naming what is measured.
repair_sequence: Name the quantity: length, mass, capacity or time.
Compare the item with a visible unit benchmark.
Estimate a sensible range, then measure and check whether the answer fits it.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner chooses a sensible unit and justifies it with a benchmark for two new quantities.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Finds perimeter by counting only some sides, counting corner points, or confusing perimeter with the space inside a shape.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner omits repeated sides, reports square units or counts grid squares inside the shape.
repair_sequence: Trace the complete outside route and label every side once.
Add boundary lengths and record a linear unit, then check the route closes.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner finds the perimeter of a new labelled shape and distinguishes it from area.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Reads the minute hand as an hour number, treats 3:45 as 3 hours 45 minutes after midnight in every context, or subtracts clock digits without tracking elapsed time.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner writes an impossible minute value, ignores morning or afternoon, or calculates 14:35 + 50 as 14:85.
repair_sequence: Read hour and minutes separately and connect analogue to digital time.
Place the start on a timeline and bridge to the next hour.
Add the remaining minutes and verify by counting the total duration.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner solves a new duration crossing an hour boundary and explains the jumps.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks a right angle must point in one direction, calls any sharp corner a right angle, or confuses parallel and perpendicular lines.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner changes classification when a shape rotates or uses parallel for lines that meet.
repair_sequence: Fit and rotate a square-corner checker.
Connect a right angle with a quarter turn.
Trace line pairs and test: same gap without meeting, or meeting at a right angle.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner classifies rotated angles and new line pairs using the defining property rather than appearance.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use a plain cream workbench, one active instrument at a time, muted route graphics and no ticking audio or celebratory motion.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace moving hands, rotating checkers and traced routes with user-controlled before/after panels, numbered boundary sides and static turn frames.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Announce quantity, value and unit together; describe clock hands separately; read timeline jumps and line relationships in a stable order.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use one action per sentence, persistent unit symbols beside full words, enlarged scales and a glossary available without covering the task.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Begin each task with 'measure or describe?', hide unused instruments and keep an estimate-do-check strip visible.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Remove timers, allow numeric entry instead of dragging, preserve intermediate measurements and provide side-by-side clocks, shapes and comparisons.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Treat estimates as ranges, retain correct sides or time jumps during repair and report which evidence needs checking rather than only marking the final answer wrong.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach measure, unit, length, mass, capacity, perimeter, duration, turn, parallel and perpendicular with objects, gestures and sentence frames.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires at least 80% across sensible unit choice, scale reading, simple perimeter, clock reading or duration, and right-angle or line-property items. Secure requires 90% with boundary-crossing time, rotated geometry and explanation evidence on two retained sessions.
evidence.parent_summary: Can choose and use sensible measures, work out simple perimeters and durations, and describe key angle, line and shape properties.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Stores quantity, chosen unit, estimate, scale reading, boundary trace, time jumps and property justification separately so broad-topic success does not hide a specific misconception.
next_actions: Route implausible measurements to benchmark and unit-choice practice.
Route incomplete boundary traces to perimeter reconstruction before calculation.
Interleave analogue, 12-hour and 24-hour times after hour and minute roles are secure.
Extend secure geometry into drawing shapes from stated properties and comparing multiple valid constructions.

qa.notes: Review-stage Year 3 measures-and-geometry pack with a deterministic 220-variant pilot bank. Four curated variants are unchanged. Generated tasks cover estimating, choosing units, reading scales, comparing and calculating length/mass/capacity, taught integer unit relationships, perimeter of simple shapes, 12/24-hour time and durations, 2-D/3-D properties, right angles and turns, horizontal/vertical and parallel/perpendicular lines, misconceptions and spaced transfer. Money remains intentionally outside this pack, matching its source note and separate number-and-money practice. Every generated task offers aligned ruler/scale/jug/clock/shape manipulatives, concrete and non-visual tables, reduced-load SEND/dyscalculia routes, alternative inputs, rich feedback and pressure-free missions without timers, streaks, lives or loss. Selected narrated contexts reference ElevenLabs assets held for human listening review; browser TTS is prohibited. Independent mathematics, accessibility, narration and renderer review remains required before promotion.
